title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Dr. Dreyfus confesses to Hercule Poirot that she witnessed ten-year-old Leopold Reynolds, the brother of the deceased Joyce Reynolds, exiting the library with a strange expression. She admits she initially withheld this information because she believed Leopold had killed his sister, but she now realizes he was likely merely shocked by the sight of her body. Dr. Dreyfus explains that she remained silent to protect the child, believing he was too young to be held morally responsible. She theorizes that the killer drowned Leopold in a stream to prevent him from testifying, as he had either seen the murderer or discovered evidence of the crime. Poirot notes that Leopold had recently been receiving large amounts of pocket money, suggesting someone was paying him for his silence.

Following this, Poirot consults with Inspector Spence and Inspector Raglan before driving to the Oak Tree School to visit Miss Emery. Poirot informs Miss Emery that he knows who killed Joyce Reynolds and presents her with a piece of paper containing four words. While Miss Emery agrees with the first two words, she finds the latter two a stretch due to a lack of evidence. Poirot connects the concept of "water" to Leopold's death by drowning, asserting that Leopold's death was not an accident but a consequence of his decision to risk his life for money. Poirot concludes that the killer is using murder as a means of self-preservation and intends to return to London to develop a plan to uphold justice and prevent further killings.
